title: cuscuta epithymum var. calliopes
intro: Cuscuta epithymum var. calliopes, also known as dodder, is a type of parasitic flowering plant that grows by attaching itself to other plants and absorbing their nutrients. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antifungal, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Cuscuta epithymum var. calliopes include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to treat conditions such as insomnia and anxiety. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
